checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is consistently derived from the source document. Headings and lists correspond to content found on pages 1, 2, 3-6, 28, 35, 37, and 38. There are no signs of invented introductory or concluding text.

I verified the phone number '203-704-0581' in the output (element index 469) against page 35 of the source PDF. No other contact information is present in the visible JSON elements. No fabricated data was found.

The conversion results are incomplete. The output explicitly contains an entry stating '28 sections omitted for token budget'. Extensive content from the RQI T-CPR Program (Pages 7-27), Cardiac Arrest Case Review (Pages 29-30), BLS Responder Education (Page 31), D-RAK Performance Objectives (Page 34 intro), and D-RAK Payment Options (Page 36) is entirely absent from the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Multiple pages and sections including: RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview (Pages 7-17), Cardiac Arrest Case Review (Page 29), and Training Payment Options (Page 36).

checks.a_analysis: I compared the tables in the source and output. The Agenda table (Page 1), Attendance table (Page 3), and Minutes tables (Pages 4, 5, 6) all have corresponding row counts in the output. The 'Curriculum Overview Comparison' table (Page 28) has 1 header and 13 data rows in both source and output (element index 357).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Within the contact paragraph for Dan Jewiss on page 35, the output omits critical information. Specifically, the email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' and the website URL 'www.TheRAKAcademy.com' are missing from the paragraph in element indices 467 and 469.
checks.b_evidence_source: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training. www.TheRAKAcademy.com
checks.b_evidence_output: Please contact Dan Jewiss at ... or 203-704-0581 with any questions or to host this training.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section containing reading_order 467 and 469, Page 35 content

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the minutes for item 11 (Page 6), the output omits the word 'Course' from the end of the formal motion text. While minor, this is a wording change in a formal government record that slightly alters the specificity of the reference.
checks.b_evidence_source: ...registration for a second NENA Center Manager Certification Program (CMCP) Course.
checks.b_evidence_output: ...fund the course registration for a second NENA Center Manager Certification Program (CMCP).

checks.a_analysis: The document was reviewed for duplicated text blocks that do not exist in the source. While headers like 'North Carolina 911 Board' repeat across the source agenda and minutes, no artificial duplication of content segments was found in the output.

The heading hierarchy in the Minutes section (starting at page 3) is broken. The section title is assigned level 1 (reading_order 14), but the subsequent major sections (Items 6, 7, 8) are assigned level 3 (reading_order 20, 25, 26) without an intervening level 2 heading. This represents a heading skip which hinders screen reader navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The Minutes section contains major headings for items 1-11.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 6 (Technology Update) is level 3 (reading_order 20) following an H1 (reading_order 14).

checks.a_analysis: The major tables, including the Agenda (p. 1), Attendees (p. 3), and Curriculum Comparison (p. 28), are structurally sound. Headers are correctly identified and column counts match the source material.

The reading order flows logically and matches the source. Items that appear at the bottom of pages (like 'Next Meeting' on page 1) are correctly placed at the end of their respective logical sections in the JSON output.

In the Minutes section (pages 3-6), the original document contains a single numbered list (items 1-11). The output disintegrates this structure: items 1-5 are placed in an unordered list (reading_order 16), items 6-8 are converted into H3 headings, and items 9-11 are rendered as standard paragraphs. This significantly changes the structural meaning and accessibility of the list.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered list items 1 through 11 in the Minutes section.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 16 (unordered list for 1-5), Reading Order 20 (H3 for 6), Reading Order 30 (paragraph for 9).

checks.a_analysis: At least one critical contact link is missing. On page 35 of the source (RAK Academy info), an email address 'D.Jewiss@me.com' is clearly present. In the output JSON, the text elements (reading_order 467 and 469) skip this email address entirely, leaving a gap in the sentence.
checks.b_evidence_source: Please contact Dan Jewiss at D.Jewiss@me.com or 203-704-0581...
